source: Care should be taken not to remove access to pods to system components which require this for their operation

input_row.title: Minimize access to create pods
input_row.description: remove create access to pod objects in the cluster. Default Value: By default
input_row.audit: The ability to create pods in a namespace can provide a number of opportunities for privilege escalation, such as assigning privileged service accounts to these pods or mounting hostPaths with access to sensitive data (unless Pod Security Policies are implemented to restrict this access) As such, access to create new pods should be restricted to the smallest possible group of users.
input_row.remediation: The ability to create pods in a cluster opens up possibilities for privilege escalation and should be restricted, where possible. Impact: Care should be taken not to remove access to pods to system components which require this for their operation
input_row.impact: Review the users who have create access to pod objects in the Kubernetes API.
input_row.reference: Where possible, remove create access to pod objects in the cluster. Default Value: By default, the following list of principals have create privileges on pod objects CLUSTERROLEBINDING                                    SUBJECT TYPE            SA-NAMESPACE cluster-admin                                         system:masters Group system:controller:clusterrole-aggregation-controller  clusterrole- aggregation-controller  ServiceAccount  kube-system system:controller:daemon-set-controller               daemon-set-controller ServiceAccount  kube-system system:controller:job-controller                      job-controller ServiceAccount  kube-system system:controller:persistent-volume-binder            persistent-volume- binder            ServiceAccount  kube-system system:controller:replicaset-controller               replicaset-controller ServiceAccount  kube-system system:controller:replication-controller              replication-controller ServiceAccount  kube-system system:controller:statefulset-controller              statefulset-controller ServiceAccount  kube-system References: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v2- privileged-access#pa-7-follow-just-enough-administration-least-privilege-principle
input_row.source: Care should be taken not to remove access to pods to system components which require this for their operation

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:** 
- The audit approach is automated because the control can be verified through the Azure Resource Manager APIs that manage Kubernetes RBAC (Role-Based Access Control).

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_aks_pod_access_restrictions_verified

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- The automated program would check the following:
  - **Azure CLI Commands or Azure Resource Manager APIs to use:** 
    - `az aks show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <aks-name> --query "roleBindings"`
    - `kubectl get clusterroles --all-namespaces`
    - `kubectl get clusterrolebindings --all-namespaces`
  
- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - Kubernetes clusters within specified resource groups
  
- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - Check for role bindings that grant `create` permissions to pod objects in the Kubernetes API. 
  - Identify specific users, service accounts, or groups that have the ability to create pods and assess their necessity.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - Pass: No role bindings with `create` permission on pod objects for non-essential users.
  - Fail: Role bindings exist that allow the `create` permission for pod objects (especially for service accounts that are not essential system components).

- **Example API endpoint or CLI command:**
  - The commands can be invoked using Azure CLI:
    ```
    az aks get-credentials --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <aks-name>
    kubectl get rolebindings --all-namespaces -o=jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.subjects[*]}{"\n"}{end}' | grep pod
    ```
